taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the lymphatic system in the text below.
textField:
The *lymphatic* system returns approximately 3 litres of *interstitial* fluid to the bloodstream daily. *Lymphatic* *capillaries* have overlapping *endothelial* cells with a *discontinuous* *basement* *membrane*, allowing easy entry of fluid and large molecules.

The *thoracic* duct is the largest lymphatic vessel, beginning as the *cisterna* *chyli* at L1-L2. It ascends through the thorax and drains into the junction of the left *subclavian* and *internal* *jugular* veins. The *right* *lymphatic* duct drains the right upper quadrant of the body.

Specialised *lacteals* in intestinal *villi* absorb dietary *lipids* as *chylomicrons*, giving lymph a milky appearance called *chyle*. *Lymph* *nodes* filter lymph and contain *B* *lymphocytes* in cortical *follicles* and *T* *lymphocytes* in the *paracortex*. In coastal India, *filariasis* caused by *Wuchereria* *bancrofti* blocks lymphatic drainage, causing *elephantiasis*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review lymphatic system features and try again.
